what_it_does: Records metadata for an image attachment in the design-source channel, pointing to a specific image asset in a presentation.
observations: The file contains only YAML frontmatter with no body content.
standouts: The target references a specific presentation image asset (p-04) via an image:// URI.
It functions as a ledger entry linking an attachment identifier to a channel-scoped image resource.
conventions: Uses YAML frontmatter for structured metadata.
Follows a channel-memory attachment schema with id, channel, attachment_type, target, and added fields.
concerns: The target URI scheme is not resolvable from this file alone.
There is no title, caption, or description for the image.
notes: A minimal channel attachment record that maps an image asset (presentation slide p-04) into the design-source channel.
questions: What visual content does p-04 contain?
Who added this attachment and why?
How is the image:// URI resolved to an actual file?
purpose_vs_actual: Stated purpose matches what it does: it is an attachment metadata entry pointing to an image target.
intention_signals: It appears to catalog image assets from a presentation within the design-source channel so they can be retrieved and tracked for provenance.
contribution: Provides a stable, timestamped identifier and pointer that a fused system can use to locate and associate the image asset p-04 with the design-source channel.
summary_for_embedding: Attachment metadata record att-800d6aaa in the design-source channel referencing image asset presentation-15p/p-04 via an image:// URI.
intention_for_embedding: To register an image from a 15-page presentation as an attachment in the design-source channel for later lookup and provenance.
